news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global economy is projected to grow by 4.5% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Advanced economies are expected to expand by 5.4% this year, while emerging and developing economies are forecasted to grow by 6%. However, disparities persist, with low-income countries struggling to recover, experiencing negative per capita income growth. The report also highlighted that global trade volume increased by 8% in the first quarter of 2021 compared to the same period last year. Despite these positive trends, uncertainties remain, such as vaccine inequality and the emergence of new variants. Policymakers are urged to address these challenges to ensure a more inclusive and sustainable recovery.
news.headline: Memo from Trump could aid Musk in uncovering social security fraud.:xxx characters

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the total number of COVID-19 cases in the country has reached 100,000, with 10,000 new cases recorded in the last 24 hours. The death toll has also risen to 2,000, with 200 new fatalities reported. In response to this surge, the government has announced stricter lockdown measures in highly affected regions. Additionally, healthcare facilities are being ramped up to accommodate the increasing number of patients. Furthermore, officials are urging the public to adhere to safety protocols to curb the spread of the virus.
news.headline: SEC denies DOGE's request for access to agency data, per Bloomberg; key details undisclosed.

news.summary: In the latest report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate has dropped to 4.3%, the lowest it has been in the past five years. This positive development is attributed to the creation of 250,000 new jobs in the last quarter, exceeding economists' expectations. Additionally, the stock market saw a significant increase, with the main index climbing by 6% to reach an all-time high of 35,000 points. Experts predict that this strong economic performance is likely to continue in the upcoming months. The government has hailed these numbers as a sign of robust recovery following the challenges posed by the global pandemic.
news.headline: Record plunge in US equity intentions reported on 04/15/2025. Read more on Bloomberg Markets.

news.summary: In recent developments, the latest job report indicates that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, which is the lowest it has been in the last ten years. The report also shows that the economy added 250,000 new jobs in the past month, exceeding expectations. Moreover, the average hourly earnings for workers have increased by 3.2% compared to last year. These positive trends in job creation and wage growth are indicative of a strong and thriving economy.

news.summary: OpenAI is reportedly developing a new social media network that will rival Elon Musk's X and Mark Zuckerberg's Meta Platforms. The network will combine ChatGPT's image generation tools and a social feed which will allow users to share AI-generated pictures. OpenAI raised $40 billion at a $300 billion valuation in a deal spearheaded by SoftBank Group and has 400 million weekly active users as of February 2025, up from 50 million in 2023. OpenAI's pivot into social media is seen as a strategic move in the battle between former colleagues Sam Altman and Elon Musk. Altman allegedly rejected a $97.4 billion buyout offer from Musk for OpenAI and instead expressed interest in acquiring X for $9.74 billion. Musk responded by calling Altman a "swindler" and later acquired X in a $44 billion deal in 2022. The platform is popular in the cryptocurrency industry, with US President Trump using it for an event. This move by OpenAI into social media signals a shift in the ongoing rivalry between Altman and Musk.
news.headline: OpenAI developing 'X-like social network', potentially challenging Elon Musk, with 400M weekly users. Musk acquired X for $44B in 2022.

news.summary: In Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands, Simpson Oil Limited, the primary shareholder of Parkland Corporation, owning 19.8% of the common shares, has released a presentation focusing on reviving performance, responsibility, and sustainable value at Parkland. news.headline: Simpson Oil, holding 19.8% of Parkland Corp shares, presents plan to address value destruction.

news.summary: On April 6, Bitcoin formed a death cross on its daily chart where the 50-day moving average fell below the 200-day moving average, historically signaling trend reversals and bearish periods. The ominous signal was seen amidst growing macroeconomic uncertainty due to tariff wars and increasing volatility. Bitcoin has experienced 10 death crosses in its history, with the 11th happening currently. The severity of these crosses varies - during bear markets, drawdowns ranged from 55% to 68%, lasting 9 to 13 months. However, not every death cross led to a bear market, some even marking local bottoms followed by rallies. Some analysts believe the current death cross could signal 6 to 12 more months of downward price action. However, others see it as a setup for a rally rather than a slide. Empirically, the Bitcoin death cross has been considered "total nonsense" and even a buying opportunity by some experts. Bitcoin is not the only asset showing warning signs, with Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 also on the verge of forming death crosses. The market currently leans towards the worse side, with Bitcoin's fate potentially tied to the performance of tech stocks. This article emphasizes conducting personal research before making investment decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin struggles despite rallying to $86K. Death cross looms with 50-day MA dipping below 200-day MA. Is bear market ahead? Analysts divided.

news.summary: In a recent update on the expanding issue, the total number of confirmed Covid-19 cases has now reached 5.2 million worldwide, with over 340,000 reported deaths. The United States remains the most affected country with over 1.6 million confirmed cases and more than 96,000 deaths. Other severely impacted countries include Brazil, Russia, and the United Kingdom. These numbers continue to rise despite efforts to implement safety measures and restrictions to curb the spread of the virus. In response to the crisis, many nations are working on developing a vaccine as well as providing support to frontline workers and those affected by the pandemic.
news.headline: New memo from Trump reportedly supports Elon Musk's efforts to uncover Social Security fraud.

news.summary: The latest report shows that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, an increase from the previous estimate of 5.3%. Advanced economies are expected to grow by 5.4% this year, while emerging markets and developing economies are forecasted to grow by 6%. Inflation in advanced economies is predicted to reach around 2.7% in 2021. The global unemployment rate is projected to decrease to 5.7% this year, with around 108 million fewer people unemployed compared to last year. Low-income countries have faced a significant setback, with an estimated 31 million more people in extreme poverty due to the pandemic. The report also highlights the importance of global vaccination efforts in achieving a sustainable and inclusive recovery.
news.headline: Investors inexperienced with market downturns are purchasing stocks despite recent drops in prices. -Bloomberg

news.summary: As Wall Street faces looming recession fears, Bitcoin's (CRYPTO: BTC) continued rally is sparking debate among investors: Is it the new digital gold or just a marketing ploy? Big banks are turning bearish at a rapid pace. JPMorgan raised U.S. recession odds from 40% to 60%, S&P Global from 25% to 35%, with Goldman Sachs and HSBC close behind at 35%-40%. This spike in recession probabilities has analysts on edge, with James Toledano, COO of Unity Wallet, warning of intensifying fears of a U.S. economic slowdown as growth projections falter. The increasing likelihood of a recession is now a top concern for market participants as global economic conditions remain uncertain.
news.headline: US recession fears intensify as big banks predict likelihood: JPMorgan - 60%, S&P Global - 35%, Goldman Sachs & HSBC - 35%-40%. Is Bitcoin a safe haven? Expert weighs in.

news.summary: US President Donald Trump is expanding into the digital assets sector with a new project blending gaming and cryptocurrency elements. The project, set to launch in late April, is reminiscent of MONOPOLY GO!, a mobile game allowing players to build in a digital city. Bill Zanker, aiding Trump’s crypto ventures, is behind this game. Zanker reached out to Hasbro for a Trump-branded Monopoly game license in May 2024. Trump, once a crypto skeptic, is now enthusiastic about Web3, with ventures like the memecoin Official Trump and various NFT projects. His crypto game faces challenges, as Web3 gaming saw a decline in daily users and investments due to macroeconomic factors like trade wars and geopolitical tensions. Trump’s shift in perspective towards crypto has raised questions about conflicts of interest and insider trading.
news.headline: Trump to launch Monopoly-style crypto game in late April with Bill Zanker, as part of his expanding crypto ventures. Web3 gaming struggles amid macroeconomic challenges.

The company is working with authorities to investigate the breach and enhance their cybersecurity measures to prevent future incidents.

news.summary: Sanofi SA provided an update on its respiratory pipeline, revealing results from the TIDE-Asthma phase 2 study of amlitelimab for treating inflammatory asthma. The study showed that while the primary endpoint of the annualized exacerbation rate at week 48 was not met at the highest dose, there were positive outcomes at medium and low doses. Amlitelimab demonstrated significant reductions in asthma exacerbations at the medium dose and even greater reductions at the high dose by week 60. Moreover, improvements in lung function and asthma control were observed as secondary endpoints, with a reduction of over 70% in exacerbations in a specific patient subgroup. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Sanofi's asthma drug amlitelimab missed the primary goal in a phase 2 study, with nominal significance at medium and low doses. Efficacy shown in reducing asthma exacerbations at medium and high doses. Subgroup saw more than 70% reduction in exacerbations.

news.summary: Aluminum producer Alcoa Corp (NYSE: AA) is expected to discuss the potential impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs when reporting its first-quarter financial results on Wednesday after the market closes. Analysts anticipate Alcoa to disclose first-quarter revenue of $3.53 billion, a notable increase from $2.60 billion in the same quarter last year. The company surpassed revenue expectations in the fourth quarter and managed to do so in five out of the last 10 quarters. Furthermore, analysts predict Alcoa to announce first-quarter earnings per share of $1.70, a significant improvement from a loss of 81 cents per share in the first quarter of the prior year. Alcoa has exceeded earnings per share estimates in the last three quarters and in six out of the last 10 quarters overall. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Alcoa to announce Q1 results; revenue expected at $3.53B, EPS at $1.70. Analysts speculate impact of tariffs on stock.

Tevogen, listed on Nasdaq as TVGN, is looking to expedite this development by teaming up with Databricks, a renowned company in data, analytics, and artificial intelligence.
